title: Fix crash when invalid COPY --from flag is specified.
body:
Closes #1894. This fixes a pretty simple issue where an invalid `COPY --from` flag is incorrectly specified. Being a new contributor I'm unsure if I should add a test case under `tests` but would be happy to supply one. This bug in particular is triggered by an invalid statement in a Dockerfile.

Signed-off-by: caiges <caigesn@gmail.com>

comments.body:
Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`
bot, add author to whitelist
Thanks @caiges, this looks great, but could you add a test for this, to make sure we don't regress in the future.
Sure thing, I'm not very familiar with bats but I think my addition should do.
@caiges thank you for your responsiveness  for this fix !

The `validate` target is failing, probably because of a formatting issue?

```
HEAD is now at 425e105d VERSION: release 1.0.0-rc8
ln -sf ../../opencontainers/runc/runc
52ef4ba - FAIL - does not have a valid DCO
1 commits to fix
Makefile:73: recipe for target 'validate' failed
make: *** [validate] Error 1
```
@caiges You need to sign each commit, also did you forget to include Dockerfile.invalid_from

@caiges You should just merge your commits together into one commit.

`git rebase -i origin`

Then after you merge
`git push --force`
Other than the missing Dockerfile, LGTM

@caiges you also need to squash (agregate) these 3 commits together.
Be careful in doing this using the next commands:
```
git reset --soft HEAD~3
git commit -m 'Fix crash when invalid COPY --from flag is specified.'
git push -f
```

> @caiges you also need to squash (agregate) these 3 commits together.
> Be careful in doing this using the next commands:
> 
> ```
> git reset --soft HEAD~3
> git commit -m 'Fix crash when invalid COPY --from flag is specified.'
> git push -f
> ```

Heh, you caught in the middle of doing so :P Cheers!
Sorry, new machine and keybase was giving me grief, good to go now :+1:

Looks like the validate still does not like the commit message.  :^(

Could you add a blank line between the heading and the rest of the content.
Looks like we might need to replay two of those jobs. Looks like intermittent connectivity issues.
LGTM
LGTM thanks a bunch @caiges !
bot, retest this please
@rh-atomic-bot r+
